revenue_by_merchant.sql:
SELECT m.merchant_name,
       SUM(t.amount) as total_revenue,
       COUNT(*) as txn_count
FROM fact_transactions t, dim_merchant m
WHERE t.merchant_id = m.merchant_id
AND t.transaction_date > '2024-01-01'
GROUP BY m.merchant_name
ORDER BY total_revenue
LIMIT 10;

issues.title: Missing filter for completed transactions
issues.problem: The query does not filter transactions by status 'COMPLETED' to calculate revenue.

issues.title: Implicit join syntax
issues.problem: The query uses implicit join syntax which is less readable.
issues.fix: FROM fact_transactions t JOIN dim_merchant m ON t.merchant_id = m.merchant_id

issues.title: Lack of comments
issues.problem: The query lacks comments explaining the logic and purpose.
issues.fix: /* Calculate total revenue and transaction count for merchants with transactions after 2024-01-01, only for completed transactions */

review.corrected_sql: SELECT m.merchant_name, SUM(t.amount) as total_revenue, COUNT(*) as txn_count FROM fact_transactions t JOIN dim_merchant m ON t.merchant_id = m.merchant_id WHERE t.transaction_date > '2024-01-01' AND t.status = 'COMPLETED' GROUP BY m.merchant_name ORDER BY total_revenue LIMIT 10;
review.summary: The query has correctness issues with missing filters and uses implicit join syntax. Adding comments would improve readability.

customer_spend.sql:
SELECT c.customer_name, c.email, c.tier,
       SUM(t.amount) as lifetime_value,
       COUNT(t.transaction_id) as total_orders
FROM dim_customer c
LEFT JOIN fact_transactions t ON c.customer_id = t.customer_id
WHERE t.status = 'COMPLETED'
GROUP BY c.customer_name, c.email
HAVING SUM(t.amount) > 1000
ORDER BY lifetime_value DESC;

issues.title: Missing Group By Clause
issues.problem: The query is missing c.tier in the GROUP BY clause, which is used in the SELECT clause.

issues.title: Incorrect Revenue Calculation
issues.problem: The query calculates lifetime value without considering the transaction status, which should only include 'COMPLETED' transactions.
issues.fix: SUM(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.amount ELSE 0 END) as lifetime_value

issues.title: Lack of Comments
issues.problem: The query lacks comments explaining the logic and purpose of the query.
issues.fix: -- Calculate customer lifetime value and total orders for customers with lifetime value over $1000

issues.title: Inconsistent Naming
issues.problem: The aliases for the aggregated columns could be more descriptive.
issues.fix: SUM(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.amount ELSE 0 END) as total_lifetime_value, COUNT(t.transaction_id) as total_completed_orders

review.corrected_sql: SELECT c.customer_name, c.email, c.tier, SUM(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.amount ELSE 0 END) as total_lifetime_value, COUNT(t.transaction_id) as total_completed_orders FROM dim_customer c LEFT JOIN fact_transactions t ON c.customer_id = t.customer_id WHERE t.status = 'COMPLETED' GROUP BY c.customer_name, c.email, c.tier HAVING SUM(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.amount ELSE 0 END) > 1000 ORDER BY total_lifetime_value DESC;
review.summary: The query has critical correctness issues with the GROUP BY clause and revenue calculation. It also lacks comments and has minor readability issues with naming.

daily_failure_rate.sql:
SELECT transaction_date,
       (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM fact_transactions f2
        WHERE f2.status = 'FAILED'
        AND f2.transaction_date = t.transaction_date) as failed_count,
       COUNT(*) as total_count,
       failed_count / total_count * 100 as failure_rate
FROM fact_transactions t
GROUP BY transaction_date
ORDER BY transaction_date;

issues.title: Incorrect Calculation of Failure Rate
issues.problem: The failure rate is calculated incorrectly because the failed_count is not correctly defined in the GROUP BY clause.
issues.fix: Use a subquery to calculate the failed_count and total_count separately.

issues.title: Potential Performance Issue with Correlated Subquery
issues.line: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM fact_transactions f2 WHERE f2.status = 'FAILED' AND f2.transaction_date = t.transaction_date
issues.problem: The correlated subquery can be inefficient as it is executed for each row in the outer query.
issues.fix: Use a JOIN to calculate the failed_count and total_count.

issues.title: Lack of Comments
issues.problem: The query lacks comments explaining the logic, making it harder to understand.
issues.fix: Add comments to explain the purpose of each part of the query.

review.corrected_sql:
WITH daily_transactions AS (
  SELECT
    transaction_date,
    COUNT(CASE WHEN status = 'FAILED' THEN 1 END) AS failed_count,
    COUNT(*) AS total_count
  FROM fact_transactions
  GROUP BY transaction_date
)
SELECT
  transaction_date,
  failed_count,
  total_count,
  (failed_count * 1.0 / total_count) * 100 AS failure_rate
FROM daily_transactions
ORDER BY transaction_date;
review.summary: The query had a critical correctness issue with the failure rate calculation and a performance issue with the correlated subquery. Readability was also improved by adding comments.
